Mean-reversion strategy using Bollinger Bands with Volume Oscillator confirmation exploits momentum exhaustion patterns when price touches lower band and volume surges, while EMA 50 acts as a trend filter to avoid counter-trend entries in strong trending markets

Combining Bollinger Bands with Volume Oscillator creates a robust mean-reversion signal by identifying extreme price deviations from the moving average, while volume confirmation filters out low-conviction trades. This approach capitalizes on the tendency of prices to revert to their mean after breaking out of tight volatility bands, especially when supported by increasing volume momentum.

Mean-reversion strategy using Bollinger Bands with Volume Oscillator confirmation captures strong momentum reversals when price touches lower band and volume spikes, filtering by EMA 50 provides trend alignment to avoid false signals during strong directional moves.
